view_banner_tip: ⚠️  Currently, the v10 alpha version is unstable, so it is recommended to use the 9.x version first.
view_home_hero_title: Highly customizable and extensible lightweight financial chart
view_home_hero_desc: A professional financial charting engine for trading terminals and finance products.
view_home_quick_create_chart_desc: KLineChart allows you to create a financial chart with just a few lines of code, while also utilizing various commonly used technical indicators built-in to make the chart look more professional.
view_home_advantage_desc: The middle section of the homepage now focuses on four key strengths: adoption speed, runtime smoothness, product capability, and extensibility.
view_home_advantage_1_desc: A shorter path from setup to a chart running inside a real product page.
view_home_advantage_1_note: A useful chart is not just one that lists many features, but one a team can integrate quickly, render immediately, and keep building on. This section focuses on low-friction adoption and a dependable first delivery experience.
view_home_advantage_1_detail_lead: The main point here is not the technical concept by itself, but whether a team can move the chart into a real page quickly. The shorter the path to first render, the cheaper it is to validate data, visuals, and interaction afterward.
view_home_advantage_1_fact_1: Useful when the team wants to launch a basic chart first, then layer in market data, indicators, and product interaction over time.
view_home_advantage_1_fact_2: It also makes collaboration easier, because product, design, and engineering can iterate around a visible interface much earlier.
view_home_advantage_1_scenario: Useful for the first integration stage, from prototype validation to production trading pages, where the team needs visible results quickly before wiring deeper data and interaction.
view_home_advantage_1_point_1: A basic chart can be integrated with only a small amount of setup code.
view_home_advantage_1_point_2: The docs, examples, and API structure make usage easier to understand quickly.
view_home_advantage_1_point_3: Works well for prototypes, internal tools, and production trading pages.
view_home_advantage_2_desc: Balances bundle footprint and interaction smoothness for real-world usage.
view_home_advantage_2_note: Once a finance chart enters production, it has to deal with longer time ranges, heavier data density, and repeated interaction. The goal here is a better balance between bundle footprint and runtime smoothness.
view_home_advantage_2_detail_lead: Once chart interaction starts to feel heavy, user confidence in the product drops quickly. The emphasis here is not a single performance number, but the overall sense of smoothness across real sessions.
view_home_advantage_2_fact_1: Better suited to real trading and market-view workflows than to demo-only environments where short sessions hide performance issues.
view_home_advantage_2_fact_2: For users who constantly zoom, pan, switch intervals, and inspect history, smoothness is part of the product itself.
view_home_advantage_2_scenario: A good fit for long-range historical data, repeated zoom and pan workflows, and real product sessions where rendering smoothness affects trust and usability.
view_home_advantage_2_point_1: Dependency-free and lightweight, so it is easier to adopt in existing web projects.
view_home_advantage_2_point_2: Remains stable and responsive with large datasets and frequent interaction.
view_home_advantage_2_point_3: A strong fit for finance products that care about interaction continuity and rendering performance.
view_home_advantage_3_desc: More than a basic chart, better suited to analysis and market-view interfaces.
view_home_advantage_3_note: The point here is not only that it can render charts, but that it already includes much of the foundation needed for professional financial interfaces. Many common requirements can start from built-in capability instead of custom work from scratch.
view_home_advantage_3_detail_lead: Feature depth determines whether the chart stays a simple rendering component or becomes a more complete finance UI capability. The stronger the built-in layer, the less duplicate work teams need during early product construction.
view_home_advantage_3_fact_1: Useful for combining market display, analysis assistance, and annotation context in one interface instead of stitching multiple tools together.
view_home_advantage_3_fact_2: For teams aiming at a more professional feel quickly, built-in capability shortens the gap between a basic chart and a serious product view.
view_home_advantage_3_metric_2_label: for richer market and analysis views
view_home_advantage_3_scenario: Useful for professional interfaces that need to combine market display, technical analysis, annotations, and trading-assistance information in one place.
view_home_advantage_3_point_1: Built-in chart styles, indicators, overlays, and axis systems cover common use cases.
view_home_advantage_3_point_2: Supports most market display, trading visualization, and analysis-assistance scenarios.
view_home_advantage_3_point_3: Suitable for growth from a basic chart module into a professional market view.
view_home_advantage_4_desc: As product needs deepen, the chart layer can grow without being rebuilt.
view_home_advantage_4_note: A chart worth adopting long term should not only work today, but also make room for future product evolution. This section highlights customization depth and the ability to grow into a core business capability.
view_home_advantage_4_detail_lead: Many products begin with a standard chart, then quickly need their own interaction logic, visual language, and analysis models. Extensibility decides whether the same chart foundation can continue serving the product as it matures.
view_home_advantage_4_fact_1: Useful for launching a standard version first, then gradually introducing custom indicators, annotations, and interaction patterns in later iterations.
view_home_advantage_4_fact_2: For long-lived products, evolving one extensible chart foundation is usually more stable than replacing solutions repeatedly.
view_home_advantage_4_scenario: Well suited to teams that already have a clear product direction, or expect the chart layer to evolve into a long-term internal capability.
view_home_advantage_4_point_1: Supports extension around indicators, overlays, figures, and axis systems.
view_home_advantage_4_point_2: Styles, interaction models, and feature combinations can keep adapting to product needs.
view_home_advantage_4_point_3: Useful both for a fast launch and for long-term evolution into a core charting capability.
view_home_case_desc: It works best as a charting foundation for finance products: fast to launch, but still deep enough for serious customization.
view_home_case_1_title: Trading terminals and pro market views
view_home_case_1_desc: A strong fit for crypto, equities, and derivatives interfaces that need more than a static chart.
view_home_case_1_point_1: Main pane, sub panes, indicators, and drawing interactions
view_home_case_1_point_2: Business-level control over styles, axes, precision, and timezone
view_home_case_1_point_3: Straightforward integration with realtime, historical, and backfill data
view_home_case_2_title: Branded finance apps and embedded modules
view_home_case_2_desc: Useful when product design, interaction detail, and bundle size all matter.
view_home_case_2_point_1: Ready for mobile gestures and browser-based delivery
view_home_case_2_point_2: Zero dependency makes embedding into Web, H5, or hybrid shells easier
view_home_case_2_point_3: Can be aligned tightly with an existing design system
view_home_case_3_title: Quant analysis and custom research tools
view_home_case_3_desc: When built-ins are not enough, extend the chart into your own analysis framework.
view_home_case_3_point_1: Register custom indicators, overlays, figures, and axes
view_home_case_3_point_2: Wrap strategy-specific interactions around your workflow
view_home_case_3_point_3: Suitable for research platforms, backtest panels, and strategy explainers
view_home_workflow_title: A clear path from integration to extension
view_home_workflow_desc: The docs already support the common adoption flow: get a chart running, wire real data, then reshape it into product-specific capability.
view_home_workflow_1_title: Initialize a chart in a few lines
view_home_workflow_1_desc: Start with the smallest integration surface and verify container, theme, and initial data.
view_home_workflow_2_desc: Use loaders, resets, and navigation controls to hook the chart into your market feed.
view_home_workflow_3_desc: Adapt colors, axes, zooming, scrolling, and precision to fit the product.
view_home_workflow_4_desc: When the product grows more specialized, register custom pieces instead of fighting defaults.
view_home_faq_desc: Most of the problems encountered during the development process can be answered from the following content.
view_home_faq_question_1: After the chart is initialized, only one line can be seen?
view_home_faq_answer_1: The chart always fills the container, checking to see if the container has height.
view_home_faq_question_2: The candle shows a line, no fluctuation, what to do?
view_home_faq_answer_2: To set the precision when setting the trading pair, call setSymbol({ ticker: 'TestSymbol', pricePrecision: 6, volumePrecision: 6 }) .
view_home_faq_question_3: How to create a real-time chart?
view_home_faq_question_4: Built-in technical indicators, calculated data is not what you want, how to do?
view_home_faq_answer_4: You can override calc by the chart method createIndicator or overrideIndicator.
view_home_faq_question_5: What if I want to create an indicator other than the built-in technical indicator?
view_home_faq_answer_5: Chart support custom technical indicators, see indicators for details.
view_home_faq_question_6: Want to mark the point of sale, how should do?
view_home_faq_answer_6: Overlays can be used. The built-in overlay has a simpleAnnotation, which can be created with the chart api createOverlay({ name: 'simpleAnnotation', ... }).
view_home_sponsor_desc: Maintaining such a chart and developing new features requires tremendous effort, which can only be sustained with the generous financial support of our sponsors.
view_local_dev_dir_explain_extension: Some module directory that can be extended
view_customize_free_tip: Contact the developer to join the development discussion group.
view_customize_sponsor_features: All services in the free version
view_customize_sponsor_tip: Donate to the project on the sponsorship page, and after completing the donation, contact the developer to get one week of answers to your questions.
view_customize_custom_features: All services of the sponsored version
view_customize_custom_tip: Contact developers to discuss specific requirements.
view_404_desc_1: Make sure the address is correct and the page hasn't moved.
view_404_desc_2: Please contact your KLineChart administrator if you think this is a mistake.
